route_trace.notes: Canvas visible within 3s of page load. Zero JS errors in console. Engine boot confirmed.
Click at canvas(0.5, 0.6) — Start Game / scene entry fallback. No crash. Engine continued rendering after click.
5 tile-area clicks at (0.3,0.4), (0.4,0.4), (0.5,0.4), (0.3,0.5), (0.4,0.5). No unhandled errors. Canvas stable.
Click at canvas(0.1, 0.1) — pause button region per view-mode-runtime-contract. No crash. Canvas present after 1500ms wait.

asset_gaps.notes: Carried from assembly manifest known_blockers. 5 of 7 obstacle sprite types present; remaining 2 fall back to placeholder. No crash observed.

asset_gaps.gap: VFX frame sequences absent (tile-disappear, wave ripple, light-chain, pierce shimmer, cascade glow)
asset_gaps.notes: Tween fallbacks active per assembly manifest. No visual error triggered during gameplay interaction test.

contract_gaps.notes: Session-flow contract step 4 specifies verifying URL contains 'game' or 'level=1'. Cocos SPA does not update the URL on scene change. Suggest: add a data-scene or data-level DOM attribute on the canvas wrapper set by GameScene.onLoad() for reliable Playwright anchoring.

contract_gaps.gap: No text-locator buttons surfaced in DOM for 'Start Game', 'Back to Map', etc.
contract_gaps.notes: All UI is rendered inside the WebGL canvas; standard Playwright text/role locators cannot target Cocos UI nodes. Automated path fell back to canvas coordinate heuristics as specified in the contract. If deeper UI assertion is needed, a lightweight DOM bridge (e.g. custom Cocos event → window.__gameState) should be added.
